subject: Computer Networking
title: How Cell Towers Work
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Cell Towers Work.

hook.text: Your phone always has signal because it's always within range of a cell tower. The whole country is covered.

buildup.text: The land is divided into cells. Each cell has a tower covering a few kilometers. Cells overlap at the edges.

discovery.text: As you move, your phone hands off from one tower to the next. The transition is seamless — you never notice.

twist.text: Each tower has limited capacity. During a concert or disaster, towers get overwhelmed and calls fail.

climax.text: Small cells add capacity in dense areas. The trend: more towers, closer together, each serving fewer users.

punchline.text: Cell towers divide coverage into manageable pieces. That's the cellular idea.

quiz.question: Why are cellular networks divided into cells?
options: Each tower covers a manageable area and hands off to the next
Towers can only communicate with one phone at a time
Regulations require exactly one tower per city block
